modules.summary: The Login module authenticates users by validating Email/Username and Password credentials and directing successful sign-ins to the Accounts Overview.
modules.where_it_fits: This module is the application's entry point for returning users on the initial sign-in page; upon successful authentication it redirects to the Accounts Overview and unlocks the left-hand navigation (Open New Account, Transfer Funds, Payments, Request Loan, etc.).
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is unauthenticated and accessing the sign-in page with a registered account credential to provide.

modules.summary: A user sign-up form that collects personal, contact, and credential information with validation rules for email, phone, ZIP, SSN, and password before creating a new account.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the initial sign-in page via the Register link, this module is part of the onboarding flow that precedes access to the account dashboard and left-hand navigation options (Accounts Overview, Open New Account, Transfer Funds, etc.). It runs during account creation and redirects back to the Login page on successful registration.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Unauthenticated visitor on the sign-in page intending to create a new customer account

modules.summary: Displays a personalized dashboard table of the customer's accounts with masked account numbers, account types, current balances, statuses, open dates, and a total balance footer.
modules.where_it_fits: Serves as the default dashboard view after a successful sign-in (Accounts Overview in the left-hand navigation). It is the primary authenticated landing page and hub from which users access features like Open New Account, Transfer Funds, Payments, and other account services.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated (logged-in) customer session with one or more accounts associated to the user.

modules.summary: Provides a form to open a new Checking or Savings account by selecting an account type, specifying an initial deposit and funding source, and validating requirements before creation.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the left-hand navigation under 'Open New Account' after successful sign-in and from the logged-in account dashboard; it is part of the account setup flow and on success redirects the user back to Accounts Overview. It sits alongside other post-login features like Transfer Funds, Bill Pay, and Manage Cards.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in and has at least one existing funding account (with sufficient balance) available to fund the new account.

modules.summary: Allows a logged-in customer to move money between their own Parabank accounts or to/from an external account with validation and transaction confirmation.
modules.where_it_fits: This module is accessed from the authenticated user's left-hand navigation as option ③ Transfer Funds and is part of post-login account management workflows, typically used after viewing the Accounts Overview dashboard. It operates alongside other account actions like Open New Account, Payments, and Account Statements.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in as a customer and has at least one Checking or Savings account available as a source; for external transfers the user has the external account details to enter and confirm.

modules.summary: Provides a bill-payment form that collects payee and payment details, validates entries (including account-number confirmation and available funds), executes payments, returns a reference code on success, and updates account balances.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the left-hand navigation as the Bill Pay (Payments) option (menu item ④) after successful sign-in and from the account dashboard; it is used during the post-login transaction flow to move funds to external payees and adjust account balances.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated customer session with at least one funded source account available and the user on the account dashboard or navigation menu.

modules.summary: Provides an interface for applying for Personal, Auto, or Home loans with amount and down-payment validation, collateral selection and simulated credit approval that returns approval or denial reasons.
modules.where_it_fits: Available from the left-hand navigation as option ⑤ Request Loan after successful login and arrival at the account dashboard (Accounts Overview). It is part of the account services flow where users submit credit applications and, if approved, a loan account is created and shown alongside other accounts.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in and on the dashboard with at least one existing account available to select as collateral and basic customer/credit profile data present in the system.

modules.summary: Provides an editable customer profile form pre-filled with name, address, and phone fields that validates input and displays success or inline error feedback upon submission.
modules.where_it_fits: This module is reachable from the left-hand navigation as "Update Contact Info" on the account dashboard that users see after a successful sign-in; it is part of account maintenance workflows alongside items like Manage Cards and Security Settings.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in as an authenticated customer and is on the account dashboard with an existing customer profile.

modules.summary: Provides customers the ability to request new debit or credit cards and to modify controls on existing cards such as spending limits, travel notices, and card status.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the left-hand navigation as Manage Cards after a successful sign-in and landing on the account dashboard (left menu item ⑦ Manage Cards). It is part of post-login account management alongside options like Accounts Overview and Security Settings for managing payment instruments and related controls.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated customer session with at least one active account; existing card(s) present if the user intends to update controls.

modules.summary: Displays a portfolio snapshot and provides forms to execute same-day buy/sell trades and to create recurring investment plans with inline validation and success confirmations.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the left-hand navigation as the Investments option after successful sign-in, it complements the Accounts Overview and other account-management features (e.g., Transfer Funds, Manage Cards) by handling investment holdings and trade activity within the user's account lifecycle. Actions on this page update the user's holdings and are reflected in portfolio data shown across the application.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in as a customer with an active account and at least one funding/destination account available for trades.

modules.summary: Provides generation of account statements for a selected period and account, and management of e-statement (paperless) preferences including email validation and success/failure messaging.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the left-hand navigation under 'Account Statements' after successful sign-in (the dashboard/Accounts Overview is the default view); it is part of the post-login account management tools alongside items like Accounts Overview, Open New Account, and Security Settings. This module is used when a logged-in customer needs historical transaction records or to change e-statement delivery preferences.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is authenticated (logged in) and has at least one active account available to select from; user is on the post-login dashboard with left-hand navigation visible.

modules.summary: Security Settings provides a form for changing the user's password with current-password verification, strong-password enforcement, match checking, and validation feedback.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the left-hand navigation as the 'Security Settings' option (item 10) on the post-login dashboard, this module is part of the account management and security area alongside Update Contact Info, Manage Cards, and Account Statements. It is used during an authenticated user's lifecycle when they need to update credential security.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is authenticated and has an active session on their account dashboard

modules.summary: Support Center provides secure message and callback-request forms for contacting customer support, with field validation, attachment handling, ticket ID confirmation for messages, and email confirmation for callback requests.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the left-hand navigation as 'Support Center' (option ⑪) after successful sign-in and landing on the account dashboard; it serves as the in-app channel for users to report issues or request callbacks within the normal account management flow.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated customer on their account dashboard with the left-hand navigation available.
